呆在家里的命令让更多的人远离道路，但全国各地的警察报告说，鲁莽驾驶的情况有所上升。
在康涅狄格州，与去年相比，交通减少了一半，但致命的机动车事故增加了约40%。
康涅狄格州警察若瑟·多雷卢斯告诉美国广播公司新闻记者吉奥·贝尼特斯，我们重新发现，随着道路开放，某些人正以此为契机，将自己的车辆推向极限。
多雷卢斯说，在冠状病毒大流行期间，他们已经看到超过限速15英里的汽车增加了90%。
下午1点收看美国广播公司。美国东部时间和ABC新闻下午4点直播。美国东部时间每个工作日都有ABC新闻团队对这部小说进行特别报道，包括最新的新闻、背景和分析。
上个月，州长高速公路安全协会(GHSA)敦促全国司机减速，因为州高速公路安全官员开始报告超速严重激增。
从3月中旬到4月中旬，加州公路巡逻队在全州范围内发布了近2500张时速超过100英里的传票，比去年同期增长了87%。
加州现在使用它的700多个电子高速公路标志来显示这样的信息：如果你必须出行，不要超速。
高速公路和汽车安全倡导者总裁凯西·蔡斯告诉ABC新闻，当你以每小时100英里以上的速度行驶时，这些撞车事故不可避免地会致命或接近致命。
在马萨诸塞州，车祸的死亡率正在上升。在明尼苏达州，与前几年同期相比，机动车撞车事故和死亡人数增加了一倍多。
专家们仍然不确定是什么促使人们从事更鲁莽的驾驶行为。
蔡斯说，有些人可能觉得自己被关在家里，认为这是一种缓解压力的方式。有些人可能一辈子都想做这件事，现在他们发现道路变得有点通畅了，他们正在利用这一局面。
GSHA负责外部事务的高级主管帕姆·沙德尔·费舍尔(Pam Shadel Fischer)表示，这可能是因为司机在没有其他司机上路的情况下更难测量自己的速度。
费舍尔说，交通量当然决定了速度，我们称之为羊群心态，这是有道理的。
在努力打击超速的同时，他们也采取了额外的安全措施，以防止冠状病毒的传播。多雷卢斯说，在康涅狄格州，他们现在交通拦截期间都戴着口罩，使用塑料袋带走司机的文件，并在可能的情况下拍摄文件。
随着国家重新开放，国家运输安全委员会主席罗伯特·萨姆沃特周一再次呼吁道路安全。
萨姆沃特说，随着我们迈向新常态，我们需要安全到达那里。不要超速。开车时不要分心。
根据GHSA的说法，现在判断一些已经解除避难所命令的州是否看到超速或鲁莽驾驶恢复到大流行前的水平还为时过早。
蔡斯说，看看当更多的人开始上路时会发生什么，这将是一件有趣的事情。当原地避难令解除后，这种危险和致命的行为是否会减少。
note_en:
Stay-at-home orders are  keeping more people off the roads, but police across the country are reporting a rise in reckless driving.
 In Connecticut, traffic has been cut in half compared to last year, but fatal motor vehicle accidents are up by about 40%.
  "We're finding that with the open roads, certain individuals are taking this as an opportunity to push their vehicles to the limit," Connecticut State Police Trooper Josue Dorelus told ABC News' Transportation Correspondent Gio Benitez.
  Dorelus said they have seen a 90% increase in cars going over 15 miles above the speed limit during the  coronavirus pandemic.
 Tune into ABC at 1 p.m. ET and ABC News Live at 4 p.m. ET every weekday for special coverage of the novel   with the full ABC News team, including the latest news, context and analysis.
 The Governors Highway Safety Association (GHSA) urged drivers nationwide last month to slow down as state highway safety officials started reporting a "severe spike" in speeding.
  The California Highway Patrol  issued nearly 2,500 citations statewide for driving over 100 miles per hour from mid-march to mid-April -- an 87% jump from the same time last year.
 California is now using its more than 700 electronic highway signs to display messages like, "If you must travel do not speed."
  "When you're going in excess of 100 miles an hour, these crashes are inevitably going to be fatal or near fatal," Advocates for Highway and Auto Safety President Cathy Chase told ABC News.
 In Massachusetts, the fatality rate for car crashes is rising. In Minnesota, motor vehicle crashes and fatalities have more than doubled compared to the same time period in previous years.
 Experts are still unsure what is motivating people to engage in more reckless driving behaviors.
  "Some people might be feeling cooped up and think of it as a stress relief," Chase said. "Some people may have wanted to do this their whole lives and now they see the roads have opened up a bit and they're taking advantage of the situation."
 Pam Shadel Fischer, GSHA’s senior director of external engagement, said it could be because it is harder for drivers to gauge their own speed without other drivers on the road.
 "Certainly traffic volumes dictate speed," Fischer said, "and there is something to be said for what we'd call that herd mentality."
  While officers try to crack down on speeding, they are also  taking extra safety precautions to prevent the spread of COVID-19. Dorelus said that in Connecticut they are now wearing masks for the duration of the traffic stops, utilizing plastic bags to take the documents of drivers and taking pictures of documents when possible.
  As the nation reopens, the National Transportation Safety Board Chairman Robert Sumwalt renewed his call for road safety on Monday.
 "As we move toward a new normal, we need to get there safely," Sumwalt said. "Don’t speed. Don’t drive distracted."
  According to the GHSA its too soon to tell whether or not some states that have lifted shelter in place orders have seen speeding or reckless driving return to pre-pandemic levels.
 "It will be interesting to see what happens when more people start going on the roads," Chase said. "When shelter-in-place orders are lifted, whether this dangerous and deadly behavior is reduced."
title: 随着高速公路交通量的下降，鲁莽的司机是否还在增加高速公路的死亡人数？
title_en: With Highway Traffic Down, Are Reckless Drivers Still Increasing Highway Fatalities?

note: 1968年的大流行是由甲型H3N2流感病毒引起的，该病毒由甲型禽流感病毒的两个基因组成，包括一种新的H3血凝素，但也含有1957年H2N2病毒的N2神经氨酸酶。它于1968年9月在美国首次被发现。据估计，全球死亡人数为100万人，美国约为10万人。大多数超额死亡发生在65岁及以上的人群中。H3N2病毒作为季节性甲型流感病毒继续在全球传播。季节性H3N2病毒与老年人的严重疾病有关，会经历定期的抗原漂移。
note_en: The 1968 pandemic was caused by an influenza A (H3N2) virus comprised of two genes from an avian influenza A virus, including a new H3 hemagglutinin, but also contained the N2 neuraminidase from the 1957 H2N2 virus. It was first noted in the United States in September 1968. The estimated number of deaths was 1 million worldwide and about 100,000 in the United States. Most excess deaths were in people 65 years and older. The H3N2 virus continues to circulate worldwide as a seasonal influenza A virus. Seasonal H3N2 viruses, which are associated with severe illness in older people, undergo regular  antigenic drift.
title: 1968年大流行(H3N2病毒)

note:
上周，Tiara Show去商场寻找夏装-这是为了分散人们对自3月份以来一直让她呆在家里的流行病的注意力。
但到处都有冠状病毒的提醒：美国鹰的一名迎宾告诉她，口罩是强制性的，并给了她一个一次性的。在里面，货架已经被削减和重新排列，牛仔裤在一个区域，衬衫在另一个区域。其他所有试衣间都关门了。在通过有机玻璃隔板支付了购买的衬衫、短裤和耳环后，Show自己从打印机那里撕下了收据。
“一切都不一样了，”这位来自蒙大拿州米苏拉的23岁球员说。“如果说有什么不同的话，那就是它让病毒感觉更真实了。”
在全国范围内，商店正在重新开业，接受一个改变了的现实。零售商多年来一直试图让顾客流连忘返，希望他们能买到比需要的更多的东西，现在他们正在重新想象他们的商店，以迎接一个充满刻意购买的抢占即走的未来。他们说，试妆或在过道里玩玩具的日子已经一去不复返了。现在的重点是让购物更快、更容易、更安全，以适应消费者预期和习惯的长期变化。
苹果公司正在门口检查购物者的体温。百思买要求顾客预约购物。梅西百货(Macy‘s)和诺德斯特龙(Nordstrom)正在取消美容咨询和改装服务，而Gap正在关闭浴室和试衣间。化妆品巨头丝芙兰将不再允许购物者测试产品。其他人在将商品重新上架之前，正在对退货进行长达72小时的隔离。
与此同时，美国鹰服饰公司(American Eagle Outfitters)正在重新想象购物体验的每一个部分。它已经投资于路边皮卡和红外线机器，这些机器可以在顾客走过时测量他们的温度。入口处的陈列品曾经堆满了服装，现在已经变成了“迎宾桌”，摆满了瓶装洗手液、一次性口罩和用来清洁鞋底的粘性蓝色垫子。衣服甚至有不同的折叠方式，以鼓励人们不插手浏览。新协议已经在435家(近一半)其美国门店推出，让我们得以一窥即使是最无害的互动也可能会如何改变。
该公司首席商务官安德鲁·麦克莱恩(Andrew McLean)说，“从你走进我们店的那一刻起，我们就想让你看到一些新鲜的东西。”“粘乎乎的垫子、迎宾桌--它们都是顾客头脑中的触发器，让他们觉得现在的情况不同了。”
这家零售商现在限制了商店的人数，当轮到顾客购物时，它正在使用一款移动应用程序来通知顾客。该公司还在一本65页的员工手册中详细阐述了新的协议，包括如何折叠牛仔裤和T恤，以便购物者在不接触它们的情况下对其进行详细检查。
这些努力表明，即使美国冠状病毒死亡人数超过92,000人，零售商仍将竭尽全力安抚紧张的消费者，让他们相信再次亲自购物是安全的。在大流行期间，已经遭受重创的行业受到了沉重打击，因为长达数周的停摆导致更广泛的经济陷入衰退。四大连锁店-J.Crew、Neiman Marcus、Stage Stores和J.C.Penney-本月已根据破产法第11章申请破产保护，第五家连锁店Pier 1正濒临灭绝。分析人士说，更多的零售商正摇摇欲坠地走向破产，这增加了他们应对公共卫生的赌注。
埃默里大学罗林斯公共卫生学院(Emory University‘s Rollins School of Public Health)全球卫生和流行病学教授罗伯特·A·贝德纳奇克(Robert A.Bednarczyk)说，“经验告诉我们，放松限制和就地避难命令意味着疾病的死灰复燃。”“口罩、体温检查、手部卫生--随着商店重新开张，这些都很重要，但增加传播的风险仍然存在。
零售商多年来一直在他们的门店增加互动展示、样品站，甚至攀岩墙和提供全方位服务的酒吧，希望为购物者提供他们在网上无法获得的体验。但分析人士表示，这些努力中的许多现在都是不切实际或不安全的，需要进行彻底改革，最终可能会降低购物体验的乐趣，并进一步削弱本已疲软的零售环境。
他们说，许多变化既是务实的，也是公开令人放心的：例如，如果有人在American Eagle要求试衣间，员工在允许他们进入之前，必须在购物者看得很清楚的情况下对门把手和固定装置进行消毒。
纽约咨询公司WSL Strategic Retail的首席执行官温迪·利布曼(Wendy Liebmann)表示：“零售商开始考虑的不仅仅是商店的清洁程度。”“他们在考虑商品销售，考虑事情的发展方向。他们怎样才能让人们购物变得更容易呢？这场大流行不会稳定下来。这将是一个漫长的过山车。
在华盛顿特区的一家独立玩具店Child‘s Play，店主史蒂文·亚伦斯(Steven Aarons)正准备减少冲动购物--减少儿童数量。他正在拆除游戏桌，取而代之的是价格更高的精灵宝可梦卡片和插孔-比如1000个拼图和滑板车-父母可以很快拿到。
Aarons在34年前开了这家店，他说：“感觉就像是我们开始了一项全新的业务。”“说我们要拿走游戏桌，不让孩子们再在商店里跑来跑去是很痛苦的，但这也是正确的做法。”
他说，可能还需要几年时间，顾客才会觉得花几个小时和他们的孩子一起浏览过道是一件很舒服的事情。与此同时，他建立了公司的网站，并增加了自大流行开始关闭以来一直需求旺盛的送货服务。员工每天从该公司的雪佛兰大通(Chevy Chase)门店减少约60份订单，尽管整体销售额下降了约50%。
在美国经营着375家门店的丹麦珠宝商潘多拉(Pandora)已经将珠宝清洁机从后台搬到了卖场，这样员工们就可以在顾客面前对试穿后的每一件珠宝进行消毒。
试戴首饰有它自己的预防措施。这家零售商已经创建了一套精心设计的系统，以确保客户和员工不会发生身体接触。顾客被指示站在离商店柜台三英尺的地方。如果有人想试戴珠宝，员工会将物品从箱子里拿出来，放在柜台上的托盘上。
该公司北美业务总裁希德·凯斯瓦尼(Sid Keswani)表示：“当员工后退时，客户会向前走一步，所以总会有6英尺高的空间。”
该公司还将客户数量限制在一次五人以内，并正在为繁忙的假日季节制定新的安全措施，假日季节约占其销售额的40%。
“我们觉得这是一次相当安全的旅行，”凯斯瓦尼说。“但我说相当安全，因为如你所知，目前还不能保证。”
数据显示，自本月商场重新开业以来，客流量稳步增长。对5月1日开业的八家购物中心(包括俄克拉何马州、田纳西州和佐治亚州)的分析显示，购物中心从涓涓细流-约为一年前购物者数量的五分之一-迅速增长，在某些情况下超过了一年前的日均价格。
根据Placer.ai的数据，截至5月6日，德克萨斯州的米德兰公园购物中心(Midland Park Mall)报告称，与前一天相比增长了79%。Placer.ai使用3000万台设备的位置数据分析了人流量模式。与此同时，南卡罗来纳州的梅西百货的客流量比去年同期跃升了9%。
美国之鹰(American Eagle)也有类似的情况，购物者一开始小心翼翼地返回，然后热情高涨。销售额也在上升：根据该公司董事长兼首席执行官杰伊·肖滕斯坦(Jay Schotstein)的说法，许多重新开业的商店销售的商品与一年前一样多。
“进来的顾客都是来买的，”他说。“他们不是来看的。”
今年3月，该公司关闭了其在美国的所有980家美国鹰(American Eagle)和Aerie门店，并让4万名员工中的大多数人休假。重新开业从5月1日开始，在医生的帮助下制定了一系列新的指导方针：员工必须在每次轮班开始前洗手20秒，然后在测量自己的体温之前对商店温度计进行消毒。
除了新的协议之外，还有更多的开放式考虑。鼓励员工从面具后面微笑，并注意非语言的暗示。“戴上口罩，一开始你可能会觉得有点尴尬，但别让这妨碍了你！”员工手册上说。“我们不希望这感觉像是一场无菌或临床互动。即使你们可能相距6英尺，但你的工作仍然是建立联系！“。
有关于自己动手制作面罩的教程，以及制作包括每位员工微笑照片的徽章的指南。高管们表示，目标是在大流行购物的时代让顾客放心。
肖特滕斯坦说：“这是一个为期两个月的过程，目的是弄清楚怎样才能让人们感到安全。”“这是一个新的现实：客户想要回来，但他们想要回到他们觉得安全的地方。”
note_en:
Tiara Show went to the mall last week in search of summer clothes — and a distraction from the pandemic that has kept her homebound since March.
  But there were  coronavirus reminders everywhere: A greeter at American Eagle told her masks were mandatory and offered her a disposable one. Inside, shelves had been pared down and rearranged, with jeans in one area, shirts in another. Every other fitting room was closed. After paying for her purchase — shirts, shorts and earrings — through a plexiglass divider, Show tore the receipt from the printer herself.
  “Everything was so different,” said the 23-year-old from Missoula, Mont. “If anything, it made the virus feel more real.”
  Across the country, stores are reopening to a changed reality. Retailers that have spent years trying to get customers to linger, in hopes they’ll buy more than they need, are reimagining their stores for a grab-and-go future filled with deliberate purchases. Gone, they say, are the days of trying on makeup or playing with toys in the aisles. The focus now is on making shopping faster, easier and safer to accommodate long-term shifts in consumer expectations and habits.
    Apple is checking shoppers’ temperatures at the door. Best Buy is asking customers to shop by appointment. Macy’s and Nordstrom are doing away with beauty consultations and alteration services, while the Gap is closing off bathrooms and fitting rooms. Cosmetics giant Sephora won’t allow shoppers to test products anymore. Others are quarantining returns for as long as 72 hours before putting merchandise back on shelves.
    American Eagle Outfitters, meanwhile, is reimagining every part of the shopping experience. It has invested in curbside pickup and infrared machines that measure customers’ temperatures as they walk by. Entryway displays once piled high with apparel have become “welcome tables” with bottles of hand sanitizer, disposable masks and sticky blue mats that clean shoe soles. Clothes are even folded differently, to encourage hands-off browsing. The new protocols, which already have been rolled out at 435, or nearly half, of its U.S. stores, offer a glimpse of how even the most innocuous interactions might be tempered.
        “From the moment you walk into our store, we want you to see something that’s new,” said Andrew McLean, the company’s chief commercial officer. “The sticky mat, the welcome table — they’re all triggers in the customer’s mind that things are different now.”
  The retailer, which now limits the number of people in stores, is using a mobile app to notify customers when it’s their turn to shop. And it has spelled out its new protocols in a 65-page employee handbook, including how to fold jeans and T-shirts to allow shoppers to examine them in detail without touching them.
  The efforts illustrate the lengths to which retailers are going to reassure skittish consumers that it’s safe to shop in person again, even as U.S. coronavirus deaths surpass 92,000. The already bruised industry has been hit hard during the pandemic, as weeks-long shutdowns ushered the broader economy into recession. Four major chains — J. Crew, Neiman Marcus, Stage Stores and J.C. Penney — have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection this month, and a fifth, Pier 1, is headed toward extinction. And analysts say many more retailers are teetering toward bankruptcy, raising the stakes on their public health response.
    “Experience has shown us that loosening restrictions and shelter-in-place orders means a resurgence of disease,” said Robert A. Bednarczyk, a professor of global health and epidemiology at Emory University’s Rollins School of Public Health. “Masks, temperature checks, hand hygiene — they’re all important as stores reopen, but there are still risks for increased transmission."
    Retailers have spent years adding interactive displays, sample stations, even rock-climbing walls and full-service bars to their stores in hopes of offering shoppers an experience they can’t get online. But analysts say many of those efforts are now impractical or unsafe, requiring an overhaul that could ultimately make the shopping experience less enjoyable and further cut into an already weak retail environment.
    Many of the changes, they say, are as much about being overtly reassuring as they are pragmatic: If someone asks for a fitting room at American Eagle, for example, employees must disinfect door handles and fixtures in clear view of the shopper before allowing them in.
  “Retailers are starting to consider more than just the cleanliness of their stores,” said Wendy Liebmann, chief executive of WSL Strategic Retail, a New York-based consulting firm. “They’re thinking about merchandising, about where things go. How can they make it easier for people to shop? This pandemic isn’t going to level off. It’s going to be a long roller coaster."
      At Child’s Play, an independent toy store in Washington, D.C., owner Steven Aarons is preparing for fewer impulse buys — and fewer children. He is removing play tables and replacing Pokémon cards and jacks with bigger-ticket items — such as 1,000-piece puzzles and scooters — that parents can quickly pick up.
    “It feels like we’re starting a whole new business,” said Aarons, who opened the store 34 years ago. “It’s painful to say we’re going to take away the play tables and not let kids run around the store anymore, but it’s also the right thing to do.”
  It could be years, he says, before customers feel comfortable spending hours browsing aisles with their children. In the meantime, he has built up the company’s website and added a delivery service that has been in high demand since the pandemic shutdowns began. Employees drop off about 60 orders a day from the company’s Chevy Chase store, though overall sales are down about 50 percent.
  Pandora, a Danish jeweler that operates 375 stores in the United States, has moved its jewelry-cleaning machines from backrooms to the selling floor so that employees can sanitize each piece after it’s been tried on, in full view of customers.
  Trying on jewelry comes with its own precautions. The retailer has created an elaborate system to ensure customers and employees don’t come into physical contact. Customers are instructed to stand three feet from store counters. If someone wants to try on jewelry, the employee takes the item out of the case and places it on a tray on the counter.
    “The customer steps forward as the employee steps backward, so there’s always that six-foot space,” said Sid Keswani, president of the company’s North American operations.
  The company also is limiting customers to five at a time and is developing new safety measures for the busy holiday season, which accounts for about 40 percent of its sales.
  “We feel it’s a pretty safe journey,” Keswani said. “But I say  pretty safe because, as you know, there are no guarantees right now.”
    Data show a steady increase in foot traffic since malls began reopening this month. An analysis of eight shopping centers that opened May 1, including in Oklahoma, Tennessee and Georgia, shows that it went from a trickle — about one-fifth the number of shoppers a year earlier — to a quick acceleration that in some cases exceeded average daily rates a year earlier.
  By May 6, Midland Park Mall in Texas reported a 79 percent jump from the day before, according to  Placer.ai, which analyzes foot traffic patterns using location data from 30 million devices. Macy’s stores in South Carolina, meanwhile, saw traffic jump 9 percent from a year earlier.
  It’s been a similar story at American Eagle, where shoppers returned cautiously at first, then with more gusto. Sales also are on the rise: Many reopened stores are selling as much merchandise as they were a year ago, according to Jay Schottenstein, the company’s chairman and chief executive.
      “The customers who come in are coming in to buy,” he said. “They are not coming in to look.”
  The company closed all 980 of its U.S. American Eagle and Aerie stores in March and furloughed the majority of its 40,000 workers. Reopenings began May 1, with a host of new guidelines created with help from doctors: Employees must wash their hands for 20 seconds at the beginning of each shift, then disinfect a store thermometer before taking their own temperature.
    Alongside new protocols are more open-ended considerations. Employees are encouraged to smile from behind their masks and pay attention to nonverbal cues. “With masks on, you may feel a bit awkward at first, but don’t let that hold you up!” the employee manual says. “We don’t want this to feel like a sterile or clinical interaction. Even though you may be 6 feet apart — it’s your job to still create a connection!”
  There are tutorials for do-it-yourself face masks, and a guide to creating badges that include a smiling picture of each employee. The goal, executives say, is to reassure customers in an era of pandemic shopping.
  “This has been a two-month process of figuring out what it’s going to take to make people feel secure,” Schottenstein said. “It’s a new reality: Customers want to come back in, but they want to come back where they feel safe.”
title: 大流行是如何改变购物的？
title_en: How the pandemic is changing shopping

note:
PubMed是由美国国家生物技术信息中心(NCBI)维护的庞大的生物医学文献数据库，是美国政府最受欢迎的网站之一，每天约有200万用户。因此，当PubMed发生变化时，它不会被忽视。
然而，对于该网站的管理员来说，不幸的是，本周公布的一项全面重新设计让许多PubMed用户怒不可遏，并在社交媒体上表达了他们有时充满诅咒的抱怨。
“难道只有我一个人讨厌新的PubMed吗？”5月19日，NCBI推出翻拍电影的第二天，埃默里大学医学院遗传学家大卫·温申克的实验室@LCNeuroScience在推特上写道。
“没有。一见钟情。瑞士洛桑联邦理工学院的生物学家大卫·苏特(David Suter)答道，这也是第二和第三，这只是数百条类似推文中的一条，这些推文很快就得到了回应-其中一些无法在家庭友好的网站上发表。到5月22日，温申克实验室的原始推文已经获得了1600多个赞。
许多投诉者谴责PubMed的新样式和布局，它显示搜索结果的方式，以及它理应增强的搜索算法。“这是你没要的新的PubMed。它会让你的眼睛流血，扼杀你的灵魂。密歇根大学医学院(University Of Michigan Medical School)分子生物学家保罗·詹金斯(Paul Jenkins)在推特上写道：#带回老朋友。
我名字的最佳匹配-前五个包括两个我不是资深作者的地方。在什么世界里这是最好的搭配呢？
--毒品猴(@Pharmgmonkeyblog)2020年5月19日。
与此同时，分子生物学家理查德·埃布赖特敦促PubMed用户要求返回到旧版本：
NCBI已经摧毁了PubMed。单击新PubMed主页右下角的反馈链接，通知NCBI您拒绝新界面，并要求永久提供旧界面。https://t.co/mhaVnCdwlj。
-理查德·H·埃布赖特(@R_H_Ebright)2020年5月20日。
其他人则提出了更微妙的看法，他们指出，几乎每一次流行网站的重新设计都会在人们学会接受之前最初受到批评。
我也讨厌新发布的东西，但现在更接近技术/网站的一面，出于许多原因需要更新&从长远来看，更新往往会让网站变得更好。只是很难重新学习一些已经成为第二天性的东西。
-希瑟·D·马歇尔(@CloudyMediaBlog)2020年5月22日。
当被要求对这样的反馈发表评论时，包括NCBI在内的国家医学图书馆的一位发言人将Science Insider引导到NLM关于重新设计的博客文章。他们指出，翻拍的目的是为PubMed用户提供现代化的界面，更容易的导航，以及基于机器学习算法的更好的搜索结果。在1月份的一篇帖子中，NCBI的客户服务总监巴特·特拉维克(Bart Trawick)指出：“无论你认为新版本的PubMed就是这样，还是你对如何改进有很好的洞察力-我们都会等待你的消息。”
这种等待显然已经结束了。5月21日，NLM主任帕特里夏·布伦南(Patricia Brennan)在Twitter上鼓励PubMed用户在其反馈表上记录他们的想法。她写道，该机构理解用户可能在过渡过程中遇到了“一些问题”。传统版本仍然可以在这里获得，尽管NCBI注意到访问它将只是短期的。
*更新，5月23日，上午11：30：本文已更新，并提供了指向PubMed&#39；传统网站的链接。
note_en:
PubMed, the massive database of biomedical literature maintained by the U.S. National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI), is one of the U.S. government’s most popular websites, with some 2 million users daily. So when something at PubMed changes, it doesn’t go unnoticed.
 Unfortunately for the site’s caretakers, however, a sweeping redesign unveiled this week has left many PubMed users fuming—and airing their sometimes curse-laden complaints on social media.
 “Am I the only one who hates the new PubMed?” tweeted @LCneuroscience, the laboratory of David Weinshenker, a geneticist at the Emory University School of Medicine, on 19 May, the day after NCBI rolled out its remake.
  “No. Hate at first sight. Also second and third,” replied biologist David Suter of the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Lausanne, in just one of hundreds of similar tweets that quickly came in response—some unprintable on family-friendly websites. And by 22 May, the original tweet from the Weinshenker lab had racked up more than 1600 likes.
 Many of the complainers decry PubMed’s new styling and layout, the way it displays search results, and its supposedly enhanced search algorithms. “HERE’S THE NEW PUBMED YOU DIDN’T ASK FOR. IT’S GOING TO MAKE YOUR EYES BLEED AND KILL YOUR SOUL. #bringbackoldpubmed,”  tweeted Paul Jenkins, a molecular biologist at the University of Michigan Medical School.
  "best match" for my name - first five includes two where I'm not senior author. In what world is that a best match?
— Drug Monkey (@drugmonkeyblog)  May 19, 2020
 Molecular biologist Richard Ebright, meanwhile, urged PubMed users to demand a return to an older version:
 NCBI has destroyed PubMed.  Click feedback link at lower right corner of "new" PubMed homepage to inform NCBI that you reject the new interface and to demand permanent optional availability of the "legacy" interface.  https://t.co/mhaVnCdwlj
— Richard H. Ebright (@R_H_Ebright)  May 20, 2020
 Others offered a more nuanced take, noting that nearly every redesign of a popular website is initially criticized before people learn to live with it.
 I also hate new pubmed, but being closer to the tech/website side of things now, updates are needed for many reasons & often make the site better in the long run. It’s just hard to relearn something that had become second nature.
— Heather D. Marshall (@CloudyMediaBlog)  May 22, 2020
 When asked to comment on such feedback, a spokesperson for the National Library of Medicine, which encompasses NCBI, directed  ScienceInsider to NLM blog posts about the redesign. They note the remake aimed to provide PubMed users with a modern interface, easier navigation, and better search results based on machine learning algorithms. And in a January post, Bart Trawick, NCBI’s director of customer services, noted that: “Whether you think the new version of PubMed is the bee’s knees just the way it is, or you have a great insight on how to make it better—we will be waiting to hear from you.”
 That wait is apparently over. And on 21 May, NLM Director Patricia Brennan took to Twitter to encourage PubMed users to record their thoughts on its  feedback form. The agency understands users may be experiencing “ some issues” with the transition, she wrote. The legacy version is  still available here, though NCBI notes that access to it will only be "short-term."
 *Update, 23 May, 11:30 a.m.: This story has been updated with a link to PubMed's legacy site.
title: 他们重新设计了PubMed，一个深受欢迎的网站。这件事进行得不太顺利。
title_en: They redesigned PubMed, a beloved website. It hasn’t gone over well

note:
近两年来，Facebook没有在面向盲人或视力低下的Facebook用户的广告上添加清晰的“赞助”标签。
这意味着，任何使用屏幕阅读器(一种大声朗读网页上的文字的软件)的人都无法区分他们朋友的帖子和付费广告之间的区别，付费广告在视觉上是在广告商的名字下面贴上一个小的“赞助商”标签。如果您使用屏幕阅读器，您将会听到的是一个字母接一个字母地拼写的胡言乱语或“赞助商”。
参议员罗恩·怀登(Ron Wyden)去年12月向Facebook指出了这个问题。在那之后，在Quartz上周向Facebook询问了这一问题后，这个问题得到了解决。
这个问题最初出现的原因显然是Facebook在2018年5月左右实施的针对广告拦截软件的长期战争中的一次猛烈抨击。
这些广告过去的标签是屏幕读者可以理解的。以前，标签的代码很简单。这只是一个链接，上面写着“赞助商”。但后来Facebook对广告的底层HTML计算机代码进行了重大修改，在标签中增加了额外的侵入性字母。因此，标签实际上不是“赞助商”，而是“Spspsononssosreredsss”。
对于在视觉上阅读网站的人来说，Facebook使用技术手段隐藏了这些额外的字母，比如将额外的文本字体大小设为零。
但全国盲人联合会(National Federation For The Blind)去年证实，JAWS屏幕阅读器应用程序会读取侵扰性的字母，克里斯·丹尼尔森(Chris Danielsen)是盲人，他是全国盲人联合会的公关总监。在他的案例中，听起来像是“Sp Sp S on S so S so S red S S”而不是“赞助商”。
后来，Facebook将“赞助商”标签的文本隐藏在页面样式说明中，这改变了标签的发音方式。在某些情况下，它是逐个字母发音的。屏幕阅读器并不是为了对抗Facebook的频繁变化而设置的，这相当于一场不对称的战争。
最近，Facebook政治广告上的“赞助商”标签不包含任何花招，所以这个模糊的标签只出现在普通的商业广告上-或者被错误描述为非政治广告的政治广告上。
解决这个问题很简单，只需去掉侵扰性的字母即可。Prime Access Consulting总裁新浪·巴赫拉姆(Sina Bahram)表示：“花了数千个小时解决这类问题后，这是一个很容易解决的问题。”Prime Access Consulting致力于实施包容性设计实践，从而产生无障碍产品。巴拉姆是盲人，他使用屏幕阅读器。
Facebook的代码把戏很可能是为了阻止旨在在Facebook上建立政治广告数据库的广告拦截程序或广告透明工具。作为第一步，这两种工具都需要检测广告；广告拦截器隐藏检测到的广告，广告透明工具将广告提交到数据库，供记者、学者和监管机构分析。这就是我注意到这个问题的原因--我曾经维护一个名为Facebook政治广告收集器的透明项目。在我们寻找“赞助商”这个词来检测广告之前(所以只有广告被提交到我们的数据库中)，我不得不构建越来越复杂的检测代码来区分广告和普通帖子，从而绕过Facebook的变化。广告拦截器也能够绕过这些变化。
“我们努力使任何人，无论能力如何，都可以访问我们的应用程序系列。Facebook发言人汤姆·钱尼克(Tom Channick)在一份声明中表示：“我们最近修复了一个用于广告标签的屏幕阅读器功能问题，我们感谢Quartz和其他公司引起了我们的注意。”
据Slate去年报道，屏幕阅读器用户在浏览Facebook时遇到了许多其他问题。巴拉姆说，在Facebook上遇到所有这些可访问性问题，包括这个问题，“真的是一次非常令人沮丧、痛苦的排他性和其他经历。”
“美国残疾人不应该成为科技公司的事后考虑。没有任何理由强迫他们花费额外的时间和精力来浏览过去的在线广告，“俄勒冈州的民主党人怀登(Wyden)说。他们应该能够像其他人一样很容易地了解为什么他们会成为这些广告的目标。“。
盲人用户也很难使用Facebook的“为什么我看到这个广告？”功能，将盲人用户排除在帮助部分解释广告商如何针对该用户查看任何特定广告的功能之外。Channick将其归结为一个漏洞，并说它也已经被修复了。
至少从2016年开始，Facebook就一直在与广告拦截软件作战，当时该公司负责广告和安培业务平台的副总裁安德鲁·博斯沃思(Andrew Bosworth)在一篇博客文章中表示，Facebook的广告是如此“相关”、“制作精良”，而且“有用”，博斯沃思写道，“我们还将开始在Facebook桌面上为目前使用广告拦截软件的人显示广告”--换句话说，用代码技巧覆盖用户的软件。
科罗拉多大学博尔德分校(University of Colorado，Boulder)研究无障碍和技术法的法学教授布莱克·里德(Blake Reid)表示，在广告上不包括易读标签“肯定违反了ADA(美国残疾人法案)的精神(如果不是文字的话)，并引发了人们对Facebook是否根据FTC法案从事欺骗性行为的质疑。”“如果他们能找到一种方法，在不干扰屏幕阅读器的情况下，做他们试图用广告拦截程序做的事情，那就太好了。这表明，在广告方面，它没有考虑清楚它将对可及性产生什么影响。“他说。
ADA要求“公共住宿场所”为残疾人提供合理的住宿条件。对于这一规定具体如何适用于网站，法院存在分歧。10月份，最高法院拒绝审理披萨公司多米诺(Domino‘s)的上诉，该公司输掉了一名盲人提起的诉讼，这名盲人不能在网上订购披萨。
Facebook确实有一支致力于无障碍问题的工程师团队；最近“纽约时报”的一篇专栏文章赞扬了Facebook使用面部识别技术自动为照片添加字幕，帮助屏幕阅读器用户听到发布的照片中的人是谁。
巴拉姆称赞了这个工具，但告诉Quartz，这还不够。他说：“就对用户体验，特别是对辅助技术用户的影响而言，还有100种其他的东西远没有那么性感，它们的实质意义要大得多。”
“当你拥有Facebook那样的资源、覆盖范围、规模和影响力时，我们就会开始承担核心责任。他们有责任确保每个人都能使用他们的平台，特别是当他们显然拥有实现这一目标的技术专长、财力、时间和人力资源的时候。“。
订阅每日简报，这是我们的早间电子邮件，包含您了解不断变化的世界所需的新闻和见解。
note_en:
For nearly two years, Facebook didn’t include a legible “Sponsored” label on ads for Facebook users who are blind or who have low vision.
 That meant that anyone using a screen reader—software which reads the words on webpages out loud—couldn’t tell the difference between their friends’ posts and paid ads, which are visually indicated with a small “Sponsored” tag under the name of the advertiser. If you use a screen reader, what you would have heard is gibberish or “Sponsored” spelled out letter by letter.
  Sen. Ron Wyden pointed out the problem to Facebook in December. After that, and after Quartz asked Facebook about it last week, the problem was fixed.
 The problem first appeared apparently as a result of one of Facebook’s salvos in its long-running war against ad blocker software, implemented around May 2018.
 The ads used to be labeled in a way screen readers could understand. Before, the code for the label was simple. It was just a link that read “Sponsored.” But then Facebook made a big change to the the underlying HTML computer code for ads, adding additional intrusive letters in the label. So instead of “Sponsored,” the label essentially read, “Spspsononssososredredsss.”
 For people reading the website visually, Facebook hid those extra letters using technological tricks, such as giving the extra text a font-size of zero.
 But the JAWS screen reader application reads the intrusive letters, Chris Danielsen, who is blind and who is the director of public relations for the National Federation for the Blind confirmed last year. In his case, it sounded something like “Sp Sp S on on S so so S red red S S S” instead of “Sponsored.”
 Later Facebook hid the “Sponsored” label’s text inside the page-styling instructions, which changed how the label was pronounced. In some cases, it was pronounced letter by letter. Screen readers  aren’t set up for what amounts to asymmetrical warfare against Facebook’s frequent changes.
 More recently, the “Sponsored” label on  political Facebook ads didn’t include any tricks, so the garbled label only appeared on ordinary commercial ads—or political ads that were mischaracterized as non-political.
 Solving the issue is as simple as removing the intrusive letters. “Having spent thousands of hours fixing these sorts of problems, this is an easy problem to fix,” said Sina Bahram, the president of Prime Access Consulting, which works to implement inclusive design practices which result in accessible products. Bahram is blind and uses a screen reader.
 Facebook’s code tricks were likely aimed to  thwart ad blockers or ad-transparency tools that aim to build a database of political ads on Facebook. Both kinds of tools need to detect ads as a first step; ad blockers hide the detected ads and ad transparency tools submit the ads to databases for journalists, academics, and watchdogs to analyze. That’s how I noticed this problem—I used to maintain  a transparency project called the Facebook Political Ad Collector. Where before we looked for the word “Sponsored” to detect an ad (so only ads were submitted to our database), I had to build more and more complex detector code to distinguish ads from regular posts, thereby circumventing Facebook’s change. Ad blockers, too, were able to circumvent the changes.
  “We work hard to make it possible for anyone, regardless of ability, to access our family of apps. We’ve recently fixed an issue with screen reader capabilities for ad labeling, and we’re appreciative to Quartz and others for bringing it to our attention,” Facebook spokesman Tom Channick said in a statement.
 Screen reader users encounter numerous other problems browsing Facebook,  Slate reported last year. Encountering all these accessibility issues with Facebook, including this one, “is a really deeply frustrating painful exclusionary and othering experience,” said Bahram.
 “Americans with disabilities should not be an afterthought for tech companies. There is no justification for forcing them to spend extra time and effort to navigate past online ads,” said Wyden, a Democrat from Oregon. And they should be able to easily learn why they were targeted by those ads, just like everyone else.”
 It was also difficult for blind users to use Facebook’s “Why am I seeing this ad?” feature, excluding blind users from a feature that helps partially explain how an advertiser targeted that user to see any particular ad. Channick chalked this up to a bug and said it, too, had been fixed.
 Facebook has been at war with ad blockers since at least 2016, when Andrew Bosworth, then the company’s VP of Ads & Business Platform, said in a  blog post that Facebook’s ads were so “relevant,” “well-made,”  and “useful” that Bosworth wrote, “we’ll also begin showing ads on Facebook desktop for people who currently use ad blocking software”—in other words, overriding the user’s software with code tricks.
 Not including legible labels on ads “certainly violates the spirit if not the letter of the ADA [Americans with Disabilities Act] and raises questions about whether Facebook is engaging in deceptive practices under the FTC Act,” said Blake Reid, a law professor at the University of Colorado, Boulder who studies accessibility and technology law. “It sure would be nice if they could find a way to do what they’re trying to do with ad blockers without interfering with screen readers. It shows a failure to think thru, on the advertising side of things, what implications it’s going to have on accessibility,” he said.
 The ADA requires “places of public accommodation” to make reasonable accommodations for people with disabilities. Courts have disagreed about how, exactly, that applies to websites. In October,  the Supreme Court declined to hear an appeal from Domino’s, the pizza company, that had lost a lawsuit filed by a blind man who couldn’t order pizzas online.
 Facebook does have a team of engineers working on accessibility issues; a  recent New York Times op-ed praised Facebook for using facial recognition to automatically add captions to photographs, to help screen reader users hear who’s in a posted photograph.
 Bahram praised that tool, but told Quartz that it wasn’t enough. “There are a hundred other things that are way less sexy that are far more substantive in terms of the impact that they would have on the user experience, especially of users of assistive technologies,” he said.
 “When you have the amount of resources, reach, scale, impact that Facebook does, then we start arriving at a point of just core responsibility. They have that responsibility to make sure that everybody can use their platform especially when they clearly have the technical expertise, the financial resources, the time and human resources to make that happen.”
  Subscribe to the Daily Brief, our morning email with news and insights you need to understand our changing world.
title: Facebook在近两年的时间里没有将广告标记为盲人广告
title_en: Facebook didn’t mark ads as ads for blind people for almost 2 years

note:
视图画廊-11张图片电动垂直起降空中出租车是我们这个时代最伟大的新兴技术之一，有望打开天空，成为无交通、高速、3D通勤路线。它们比直升机旅行安静得多，也便宜得多，还将使用当地零排放的电力，许多车型表明，它们的每英里费用与拼车费用大致相同。
最终，市场似乎一致认为，它们将是无人驾驶的机器人，甚至比最早的有人驾驶的版本更便宜、更可靠。如果机上的自动驾驶电脑混乱，远程操作员将接管并挽救局面，就像他们在驾驶一架Mavic无人机一样，每离开一名飞行员就会成为空中的一个额外乘客座位。
大量的eVTOL空中出租车将改变城市和生活方式的设计方式。写字楼、火车站和最后一英里运输站顶部的天窗将鼓励多种方式的通勤。风景秀丽的沿海地区的房地产可能会蓬勃发展，因为人们把在郊区车流中爬行45分钟换成了每小时120英里(200公里/小时)的45分钟航空旅行，并决定住在离办公室更远的地方。
几家公司告诉我们，他们相信他们可以提供空中出租车服务，价格与乘坐优步的价格大致相同。请注意，这个假设是中长途旅行，乘坐40英里(64公里)的优步并不便宜。
这项技术感觉并不遥远。电动多旋翼提供出色的悬停稳定性，这要归功于电机产生的巨大、近乎瞬间的扭矩，当风力条件移动时，电机可以非常迅速地做出反应，以稳定飞机。关于这些机器应该如何建造，有太多不同的观点。
有些设计就像带客舱的大型多旋翼一样简单。其他人试图通过倾斜旋翼和多模式飞行的形式增加复杂性来扩大航程；它们像多旋翼一样盘旋，过渡到机翼辅助前飞，这要高效得多，但在动力学上更复杂。
第一个问题与阻碍高性能电动摩托车的问题相同：目前的锂电池技术还不能让你携带足够的能量。在能量密度至少翻一番之前，这些设计中的大多数都不能提供足够长的续航时间，使它们在商业上可行。这些大电池组也需要充电，这将使飞机停飞很长一段时间。
二是认证。在这个阶段，我们不知道有任何eVTOL设计被认证为完全可商业操作的飞机，虽然一些努力正在想方设法推动法律的边缘，但事实仍然是，这些都是全新的飞机类别，认证、测试和监管过程将极其昂贵和耗时。
第三个影响第二个：安全。电动垂直起降飞机可以提供其他飞机无法比拟的各种冗余选项。拿出一个螺旋桨或马达，甚至几个，这些设计中的大多数仍然可以飞行，这要归功于分布式推进和智能软件。弹道降落伞可以发射，以便在完全灾难性故障的情况下轻轻地将其击落。在绝大多数情况下，这些东西应该是非常非常安全的。
但这就是问题所在；弹道降落伞只能救你到一定高度以上，也许120英尺左右。在这下面，他们没有时间打开，这意味着每次你起飞或降落在这些机器中，你都会暴露在一个时间窗口里，在这段时间里，整个系统故障会像石头一样把你摔下来。
我们一直把离地面30到120英尺的区域称为“死亡地带”，我确信电子产品制造商希望我们不要再谈论这件事了。我们私下听说过这个问题的一些相当疯狂的解决方案，但到目前为止还没有人站出来说他们已经解决了这个问题。
沿着轨道下去，如果天空要安全地容纳大量这样的机器在散布在市区周围的几个空中窗口之间穿梭，空中交通管制也必须有相当大的飞跃。
所有这些问题都在研究中，我们认为现在是快速总结正在研究机身并引领这一新兴领域的主要参与者的好时机。以下是我们所看到的主要竞争者。
过渡eVTOL，6个倾斜转子，5个座位，最高时速200英里，航程150英里。
还记得乔比大猩猩吗？嗯，发明三脚架的人乔本·贝维尔特(JoeBen Bevirt)早就离开了弯曲三脚架行业。10多年来，他和一支由疯狂的航空科学家组成的顶尖团队一直与世隔绝在圣克鲁斯郊外一个杂乱无章、略显嬉皮的院落里，致力于壮观的eVTOL设计。该公司目前在北加州拥有几家工厂。
卓比航空最近完成了5.9亿美元的巨额融资，其中大部分来自丰田，使其筹集的资金总额达到7.2亿美元。虽然它在其存在的大部分时间里都是在隐形模式下运作，但该公司已经建造并飞行了其华丽飞机的全尺寸、五座原型飞机，其六个倾斜的旋翼和V形双尾。Joby说，它在几乎完全安静的情况下以每小时200英里(322公里/小时)的速度飞行(无论如何，一旦它向前飞行)，并且使用电流电池每次充电可以提供超过150英里(240公里)的续航里程。
Joby表示，它于2018年开始了机身认证过程，预计将在2024年之前的某个时候完成。随着丰田提供大规模制造援助以及堆积如山的现金，Joby处于有利地位，有望成为领先力量。我们悄悄地想，随着这一切的发展，丰田在氢燃料电池方面的专业知识是否会带来优势--在商业航班开始时，拥有巨大续航里程和近乎即时加油能力的高速倾斜转子燃料电池eVTOL看起来可能比电池充电器上的长时间停机时间更具吸引力。
该公司已经与优步签署了多年的商业合作伙伴关系，并正在考虑在多个市场建立自己的按需空中出租车服务。
过渡eVTOL，在倾斜的河岸上有36个管道风扇，5个座位，最高时速186英里，射程186英里。
总部位于德国慕尼黑郊外的Lilium是另一家拥有超过3.4亿美元资金和350名员工的大公司，正准备将规模扩大到1000人以上。百合的飞机无疑是该领域中最漂亮的飞机之一，它是一个超级跑车风格的机舱，机身之间是纤细的锤头机翼，两边是非常紧凑的电动喷气式飞机，没有暴露在外面的笨拙的螺旋桨。这是一个极具未来主义风格的设计。
它也在飞行，早期悬停和机动测试的公开测试视频可以在网上获得，高速测试很快就会随之而来。这款喷气式飞机的最高时速约为186英里(300公里/小时)，使用现有电池技术的航程可达186英里(300公里)，令人印象深刻。大量的小风扇使其在电机故障时具有极好的冗余性。
Lilium几个月前告诉我们，它看到了一条非常清晰的获得认证的道路，该公司计划在2025年推出自己的服务。在这个阶段，它似乎对向其他运营商出售飞机不感兴趣。
Skai&39；最近的崛起引起了轩然大波，因为该公司决定放弃锂电池，转而采用液氢动力总成，彻底消除了电池技术瓶颈，而几乎所有其他公司都希望，到推出商业服务时，电池技术瓶颈将得到解决。
一个400升的液氢罐将给这台机器提供大约400英里的续航里程，Skai将在几分钟内从关键仓库的氢气罐开始加满它，而锂飞机坐在那里充电，一次停止服务几个小时。因为航程是如此全面而不是问题，Skai不会费心于机翼或过渡能力来使其飞机更有效率。取而代之的是，它只是一个简单的大六角飞行器，有一个5个座位的机舱，该公司表示，这种复杂性的大幅降低应该有助于它更快地通过监管机构。
SKAI总部设在美国东海岸的新英格兰，现阶段由一家私人投资者提供支持。该公司去年年中告诉我们，它预计将在2020年获得认证，并希望在其他任何人做好准备之前，通过自己的应用程序和向其他运营商销售产品，超越竞争对手进入服务领域。虽然大多数公司都在计划Skyport到Skyport的穿梭业务，但Skai希望在空间允许的情况下能够降落在你的后院接送。
在距离德国斯图加特不远的地方，Volocopter很早就推出了一款简单的双座VC200多用途飞机，这要追溯到2013年，当时该公司还叫E-Volo。更早些时候，如果你还记得2011年第一次看到一些疯子驾驶底部有瑜伽球的大型多旋翼飞机，那就是2011年的这些家伙。真正的莱特兄弟级别的航空先锋狂热。
现在，该公司的投资略高于1.3亿美元，已经完成了迪拜、斯图加特和新加坡的示范飞行，后者有一名飞行员在飞机上。VoloCity空中出租车很有吸引力，但它的雄心有点谦逊。它的18个转子位于两座机舱顶部的六个分支臂上。它实际上是一架大型的载人多旋翼飞机，最高时速68英里，航程22英里，或多或少是一架专用于拥挤城市短途旅行的城内飞机。
该公司还在开发自己品牌的城市天港，以及能够携带200公斤(440磅)有效载荷的货运无人机版本的飞机。
亿航总部位于中国南方贵州省，最初只是一家普通的摄像无人机公司，但在2016年CES上凭借一款全面打造的单座载人无人机原型在eVTOL领域崭露头角。据我们所知，该公司在纳斯达克证券交易所上市后，已经筹集了9200万美元多一点的资金。
亿航已经在几个国家进行了多次试验和示范飞行，包括许多载人飞行。它的216机身使用16个旋翼，安装在8个臂同轴上，并展示了它在关闭多个道具的情况下飞行的能力。这被设想为一种无人驾驶空中出租车服务，乘客只需在触摸屏上进入他们要前往的Skyport目的地，飞机就会自动飞行，远程操作员银行随时准备在出现任何问题时接管控制权。
和Volocopter一样，它也是一种短程双座设计。但亿航希望在其垂直机场中心实现疯狂之类的自动化，拥有多级自动化飞机仓储、自动充电和折叠双臂，以实现高密度停车。
陀螺仪有一个大的主旋翼和四个前旋翼，五个座位，最高时速175英里，未知航程。
没有一款eVTOL的设计比贾阿姨的罗莎在评论区引起了更多的争议-但事实是，它的设计比任何一款多旋翼新款都要古老得多，经过测试和成熟的设计。它看起来像一架直升机，但它不是。它是一架慢旋翼陀螺仪，贾阿姨买下了CarterCopter业务的版权，该业务有1000多次起降和100多个小时的飞行测试。
JOUNT正在制造一款电池电动版本，将提供安静、快速的五座空中出租车，这款机器与其他领域相比有两个致命的优势。首先，作为一个陀螺仪，没有什么特别新的或复杂的认证过程。不需要创建新的类别，它只需根据现有的联邦航空局第29部分旋翼机法规进行认证和飞行即可。
其次，与其他eVTOL相比，这个巨大的顶部转子看起来有点笨拙，它提供了在整个系统发生故障的情况下，从任何高度自动旋转到受控着陆的能力。在我们看来，这使它成为我们所见过的所有eVTOL设计中最安全的，而且它还有其他的诀窍，特别是在装配式驾驶舱的形式上，这可能会让这次旅行比大多数人都舒适。
根据其创始人的说法，短途旅行是自筹资金的，而且有足够的资金进入下一阶段。该公司希望在2023年之前获得认证，并在2025年之前提供飞行商业服务。当然，这是一家值得关注的公司，即使很多人不希望未来的3D空中出租车是这样的。
带有12个VTOL转子和一个推杆的过渡多视镜，两个座位，最高时速100英里，航程25英里。
在谷歌联合创始人拉里·佩奇(Larry Page)的支持下，Kitty Hawk已经将其自动驾驶的双座空中出租车剥离出来，成立了一家名为Wisk Aero的独立企业，由波音公司(Boeing)共同拥有。科拉机身看起来很像一架飞机，它有12个轻快倾斜的垂直起降道具，这些道具的形式是额外的比特，当飞机使用其巨大的后部推进器道具转换到向前推力时，这些道具的设计是为了提供低阻力。测试已经很充分和成功了。
值得注意的是，Wisk计划从第一天起让Cora自主运行，第一天可能很快就会到来。这是因为Wisk抓住了新西兰航班代码中的一个漏洞，民航局第115部分的冒险航空法规提供了一条途径，让Cora可以开始商业飞行，而需要通过的繁文缛节比其他任何地方都少得多。
航空巨头波音公司(Boeing)是世界上拥有第13大资金来源的公司(拉里·佩奇)，这家公司的资源可能像海洋一样深，但从招聘名单的长度来看，它的进展速度要比莉莉姆(Lilium)或乔比(Joby)要谨慎得多。(比方说，比起莉莉姆(Lilium)或乔比(Joby))，这家公司的资源可能会像海洋一样深。
空客已经建造了两款关键的eVTOL候选机型-单座过渡8旋翼Vahana和大型ol4座同轴8旋翼CityAirbus。然而，这两个平台都只是技术验证平台；Vahana项目于2019年底停止，这里的CityAirbus可能也会在一两年内失效，因为它已经试飞了数百次。
因此，该公司相当平淡无奇的规格表--110千瓦时电池的飞行距离只有15分钟，巡航速度为120公里/小时--不太可能表明空客在幕后真正在做什么，也不太可能表明当空客不可避免地卷入商业层面的空中出租车游戏时，该公司可能会带来什么。我们可以想象，空客将在未来几年的某个时候推出一款更大的过渡型eVTOL。
以色列的“城市之鹰”可能不会出现在这份名单上，因为它不是电动的，但这种怪异的设计最终会改用氢燃料电池系统，而且它肯定是一架有趣的飞机。它基于战术机器人公司(Tactical Robotics)的Cormorant机身，前身为AirMule，该公司已经进行了大量测试，以验证其奇怪的处理方式。
目前的涡扇驱动的CityHawk设计使用两个大的，完全屏蔽的，据称旋转缓慢的风扇，在一个大致汽车形状的车身的前部和后部来处理升降机。至少在Cormorant上，前方推力是由后方的一对额外的水平管道风扇提供的。我们有点不明白它是如何使用这种配置来平衡自己的，在这种配置下，推力源似乎沿着一个单一的轴组合在一起，而不是像大多数设计中那样变得很宽，但它肯定看起来能飞起来，即使演示飞行看起来有点摇晃。
值得注意的是，这个设计可以让6个人登机，占用的空间比这个列表上的任何其他设计都要小得多。它的宽度与一辆汽车差不多，如果稍长一点，城市航空公司表示，占地面积小将使这款车有更广泛的着陆点选择，以及将许多着陆点紧密停放在一起的能力。
转向氢燃料电池动力系统，这在路线图上非常重要，将允许两端有两个反向旋转的风扇，并应该会大大增加航程，本质上是一种没有机翼辅助的类似无人机的设计。声称的最高时速168英里似乎有点乐观，但我们将拭目以待。
奥罗拉飞行科学公司(Aurora Flight Sciences)及其PAV客机(PAV Passenger Air Vehicle)现在由波音公司全资拥有，这是波音公司进军eVTOL蛋糕的又一根手指，尽管其设计与Wisk&39；的Cora惊人地相似。同样，它看起来很像一架内置了VTOL系统的飞机，尽管这一次VTOL道具在几乎看起来像一套着陆滑板上从前到后运行，机翼没有束缚。推杆道具整齐地插在后面。
和科拉一样，它的构思是完全自主的，奥罗拉也有两座和四座的版本正在开发中。这就是我们目前所知的情况，除了奥罗拉已经与Uber Elevate项目签约合作之外，名单上还有其他一些名字。
转换式10旋翼多翼飞机，配有两个前支柱，2/4个座位，最高时速155英里，航程186英里。
你可能知道Hoversurf是天蝎气垫车背后的俄罗斯精神病患者组成的团队，但这家公司的雄心不仅仅是骑着相当于越野自行车的空中弹射上天。最新的Project Formula设计与上面的超酷跳汰机略有不同，主要是它现在使用了10个更大的垂直起降导管风扇和一对向前飞行的推进器道具。根据需要的射程数字，也有有翼和无翼版本的计划。
Hoversurf将倾斜道具视为对健康的危害，占V-22鱼鹰事故的90%，因此它乐于将垂直推力和向前推力完全分开。着陆滑板容纳垂直起降道具的方式，它们应该在向前飞行时体面地避开。速度和射程数据清楚地假设你正在谈论有翼版本，而该公司在这一点上似乎并没有在小型无人机大小的原型上取得太大进展。
带有四个倾斜转子、六个座位、最高时速150英里、射程62英里的过渡多视镜。
贝尔公司总部设在得克萨斯州，是一家主要的飞机制造商，在火箭飞机和战斗机方面有着著名的历史，在直升机业务方面也有近80年的历史。自1960年以来，它一直是德事隆的子公司，德事隆还拥有Beechcraft、Hawker和Cessna，年收入超过130亿美元。除了生产市场上一些最知名的军用和民用直升机外，它还是V22鱼鹰的合作伙伴，以及许多其他倾斜旋翼项目。你最好相信贝尔在攻击一辆空中出租车。
Nexus4EX是我们见过的最新设计，它是一台大而时尚的机器，使用了一个大机翼和四个大的、有遮盖的、倾斜的转子。它的续航能力不仅限于电池；目前，它还震撼了一台燃气轮机发电机。该公司表示，它认为包括混合动力车在内的多种动力总成选项将使其成为对商业客户更具吸引力的产品。
像这样的设计，卡雷姆蝴蝶和CityAirbus有点令人担忧，因为它们在发动机故障的情况下不提供冗余。随着事态开始升温，我们将拭目以待。
具有8个VTOL转子和2个前置道具、5个座位、未知最高速度、未知范围的过渡多视镜
巴西航空工业公司(Embraer)是一家大型制造商，主要生产商用、军用和商务机，每年的收入为30-40亿美元。因此，虽然它不是波音公司，但它是一家拥有51年历史和大量运营经验的老牌公司。巴西航空工业公司(EmbraerX)总部设在佛罗里达州，专门为打入DISR而成立。

note_en:
View gallery - 11 images   Electric VTOL air taxis are one of the great emerging technologies of our time, promising to unlock the skies as traffic-free, high-speed, 3D commuting routes. Much quieter and cheaper than helicopter travel, they'll also run on zero-local-emission electric power, and many models suggest they'll cost around the same per mile as a ride share.
 Eventually, the market seems to agree, they'll be pilotless automatons, even cheaper and more reliable than the earliest piloted versions. Should the onboard autopilot computers get confused, remote operators will take over and save the day as if they're flying a Mavic drone, and every pilot gone will be an extra passenger seat in the sky.
 Large numbers of eVTOL air taxis will change the way cities and lifestyles are designed. Skyports atop office buildings, train stations and last-mile transport depots will encourage multi-mode commuting. Real estate in scenic coastal areas might boom as people swap 45 minutes crawling along in suburban traffic for 45 minutes of 120 mph (200 km/h) air travel, and decide to live further from the office.
 Several companies have told us they believe they can deliver an air taxi service for about the same price as it would cost to take an Uber. Mind you, this assumes medium to long distance trips, and it's not exactly cheap to take a 40-mile (64 km) Uber ride.
  The technology doesn't feel far away. Electric multirotors deliver excellent hover stability thanks to the enormous, near-instant torque production of electric motors, which can respond very quickly to stabilize an aircraft as wind conditions move it around. And there are a plethora of different takes on how these machines should be built.
 Some designs are as simple as big multirotors with passenger cabins. Others attempt to extend range by adding complexity in the form of tilting rotors and multi-mode flight; hovering like a multirotor, they transition to wing-assisted forward flight, which is much more efficient but dynamically more complicated.
   The first is the same problem that's holding back high-performance electric motorcycles: current lithium battery technology simply doesn't allow you to carry enough energy yet. Until energy density is at least doubled, most of these designs don't offer range endurance long enough to make them commercially viable. These big battery packs will need charging, too, which would ground the aircraft for a significant time.
 The second is certification. At this stage, we're not aware of any eVTOL design that's certified as a fully commercially operable aircraft, and while some endeavors are finding ways to push forward around the edges of the law, the fact remains that these are entirely new categories of aircraft, and the process of certifying, testing and regulating them is going to be monstrously expensive and time consuming.
 The third impacts the second: safety. Electric VTOL aircraft can offer all sorts of redundancy options that no other aircraft can match. Take out a prop, or a motor, or even a few of them, and most of these designs can still fly thanks to distributed propulsion and smart software. Ballistic parachutes can be fired to bring them down gently in the case of total catastrophic failure. In the vast majority of cases, these things should be very, very safe.
  But here's the issue; ballistic parachutes can only save you above a certain altitude, maybe 120 feet or so. Below that, they don't have time to open up, which means that every time you take off or land in one of these machines, you're exposed to a window of time in which total system failure would drop you like a stone.
 We've been calling the area between 30 and 120 feet off the ground the "death zone," and I'm sure eVTOL manufacturers wish we'd stop talking about it. We have heard, off the record, about some pretty wild solutions to this problem, but as of yet nobody has come forward to say they've solved it.
 Down the track, there will also have to be a considerable leap forward in air traffic control if the skies are going to safely hold large numbers of these machines zipping about between a bunch of skyports dotted around an urban area.
 All these problems are being worked on, and we thought now was a good time to do a quick round-up of the major players that are working on airframes and leading this emerging field. Here are the major contenders as we see it.
  Transitioning eVTOL with six tilting rotors, five seats, 200 mph top speed, 150 mile range
  Remember the Joby Gorillapod? Well, the fellow that invented it, JoeBen Bevirt, has long left the bendy tripod business. For more then 10 years, he and a crack team of mad aviation scientists have been sequestered away on a sprawling, slightly hippie compound outside Santa Cruz, working on spectacular eVTOL designs. The company now has several facilities spread out across Northern California.
 Joby Aviation has recently completed  a monster US$590 million fundraising round, much of that coming from Toyota, bringing the total it's raised to US$720 million. While it's operated in stealth mode for most of its existence, the company has built and flown full-size, five-seat prototypes of its gorgeous aircraft with its six tilting rotors and V-shaped double tail. Joby says it flies at up to 200 mph (322 km/h) in near-total silence (once it's flying forward, anyway) and offers over 150 miles (240 km) of range per charge on current-gen batteries.
 Joby says it commenced the airframe certification process in 2018, and expects it to be complete sometime before 2024. With Toyota contributing mass manufacturing assistance as well as heaping piles of cash, Joby is well positioned to be a leading force. We quietly wonder whether Toyota's expertise with hydrogen fuel cells may offer an advantage as this all plays out – a high-speed tilt-rotor fuel cell eVTOL with enormous range and near-instant refueling could look a lot more attractive than long out-of-service hours on a battery charger when commercial flights begin.
 The company has signed a multi-year commercial partnership with Uber, and is also looking at setting up its own on-demand air taxi service in multiple markets.
  Transitioning eVTOL with 36 ducted fans on tilting banks, five seats, 186 mph top speed, 186-mile range
  Lilium, based outside Munich in Germany, is another major player with more than US$340 million behind it and a workforce of 350 people that's preparing to scale up past 1,000. Lilium's aircraft is without doubt one of the most beautiful in the sector, a supercar-style cabin between slim, hammerhead wings with banks of very compact "electric jets" and no ungainly exposed propellers. It's a superbly futuristic design.
 It's also flying, with public test videos available online of early-stage hover and maneuvering tests, and high speed testing soon to follow. The jet offers a top speed around 186 mph (300 km/h) and an impressive range of 186 miles (300 km) using existing battery technology. The huge number of small fans give it excellent redundancy in case of motor failure.
 Lilium told us a few months ago it sees " a pretty clear path towards certification," and the company is targeting 2025 for the launch of its own service. At this stage it doesn't appear to be interested in selling aircraft to other operators.
    Skai's recent emergence has thrown a cat amongst the pigeons, because its decision to ditch lithium batteries for a liquid hydrogen powertrain completely eliminates the battery technology bottleneck that almost every other company is hoping will resolve itself by the time they launch commercial services.
 A 400-liter liquid hydrogen tank will give this machine a range around 400 miles, and Skai will fill it up in minutes from hydrogen tanks at its key depots while the lithium aircraft sit and charge, out of service for hours at a time. Because range is so comprehensively not an issue, Skai isn't bothering with wings or transitioning capabilities to make its aircraft more efficient. Instead, it's just a simple big hexacopter with a five-seat cabin, and the company says this great reduction in complexity should help get it across the line quicker with regulating bodies.
 Based in New England on the East Coast of the USA, Skai is supported at this stage by a single private investor. The company  told us midway through last year that it's expecting to be certified in 2020 and hopes to leapfrog its competitors into service, both through its own app and selling to other operators, before anyone else is ready. And while most companies are planning skyport-to-skyport shuttle operations, Skai wants to be able to land in your back yard for pickups and dropoffs if space allows.
    Not far outside Stuttgart, Germany,  Volocopter was pretty early on the scene with a simple 2-seat VC200 multicopter flying  way back in 2013, when the company was still called E-volo. Earlier still, if you remember the first time you saw some madman flying a big multirotor with a  yoga ball on the bottom, that was these guys back in 2011. Genuine Wright Brothers level aviation pioneer craziness.
 Now, with a little over US$130 million in investment, the company has completed demonstration flights over Dubai, Stuttgart and Singapore, the latter with a pilot on board. The VoloCity air taxi is attractive, but somewhat humble in its ambitions. Its 18 rotors sit on six branching arms coming off the top of the two-seat cabin. Effectively a big ol' manned multirotor, its 68 mph top speed and 22-mile range make it more or less an exclusively intra-urban aircraft for short trips in crowded cities.
 The company is also working on its own brand of urban skyport, as well as a cargo drone version of the aircraft capable of carrying a 200 kg (440-lb) payload.
    Headquartered in Guizhou, Southern China,  eHang started out as a regular ol' camera drone company, but burst onto the eVTOL scene at  CES 2016 with a fully built prototype single-seat manned multicopter. Best we can tell, the company has raised a little over US$92 million after listing on the NASDAQ stock exchange.
 eHang has staged numerous test and demonstration flights in several countries, including many manned flights.  Its 216 airframe uses 16 rotors, mounted coaxially on 8 arms, and has demonstrated its ability to fly with multiple props switched off. This is conceived as a pilotless air taxi service, in which passengers will simply enter the skyport destination they're headed for on a touchscreen, and the aircraft will fly there autonomously, with banks of remote operators standing ready to take over control in case of any issues.
 Like the Volocopter, it's a short-range, two-seat design. But eHang wishes to automate things like crazy at its "vertiport" centers, with multi-level automated aircraft storage, automatic charging and fold-up arms for high-density parking.
  Gyrodyne with one large main rotor and four forward rotors, five seats, 175 mph top speed, unknown range
  No eVTOL design has raised more controversy in the comments section than  Jaunt's ROSA – but the fact is, it's based on a much older, tested and established design than any of the multirotor newcomers. It looks like a helicopter, but it isn't. It's a slow-rotor gyrodyne; Jaunt bought up the rights to the CarterCopter business, which has over a thousand takeoffs and landings, and more than 100 hours of flight testing under its belt.
 Jaunt is building a battery-electric version that will deliver quiet, fast five-seat air taxis, and this machine has two killer advantages over the rest of the field. Firstly, as a gyrodyne, there's nothing particularly new or complicated about the certification process. No new category has to be created, it can simply be certified and flown under existing FAA Part 29 rotorcraft regulations.
 Secondly, that big top rotor, which looks a bit dorky compared to other eVTOLs, offers the ability to autorotate down to a controlled landing from any height in case of total system failure. This makes it, in our opinion, the safest of all the eVTOL designs we've seen, and it has other tricks up its sleeve, notably in the form of a gimballed cockpit that could make this a comfier ride than most.
 Jaunt is "self-funded, and sufficiently funded to get to the next level" according to its founders. The company hopes to be certified by 2023 and flying commercial services by 2025. Certainly a company to keep an eye on, even if a lot of people don't want this to be what a future 3D air taxi looks like.
  Transitioning multicopter with 12 VTOL rotors and a pusher prop, two seats, 100 mph top speed, 25 mile range
  Famously backed by Google co-founder Larry Page,  Kitty Hawk has spun off its self-piloting, 2-seat air taxi into a separate business called Wisk Aero, co-owned by Boeing. The Cora airframe looks a lot like a plane with extra bits in the form of twelve jauntily tilted VTOL props designed to give low drag when the aircraft shifts to forward thrust using its large rear pusher prop. Testing has already been plentiful and successful.
 Notably, Wisk is planning to make Cora autonomous from day one, and day one could be fairly soon. That's because Wisk has pounced on a loophole in the New Zealand flight codes, where the Civil Aviation Authority's Part 115 Adventure Aviation regulations offer a pathway through which the Cora may begin flying commercially with far less red tape to clear than anywhere else.
 With aviation giant Boeing and the 13th-deepest single pocket in the world (Larry Page's) to dip into, this company's resources could be as deep as the ocean, but judging by the length of its recruiting list, things are proceeding at a considerably more cautious pace than, say, Lilium or Joby.
    Airbus has built two key eVTOL candidates – the  single-seat transitioning 8-rotor Vahana, and the big ol'  4-seat coaxial 8-rotor CityAirbus. Both, however, have simply been technology validation platforms; the Vahana program was discontinued at the end of 2019, and the CityAirbus here will probably outlive its usefulness within a year or two as well, having already flown hundreds of test flights.
 Thus, its fairly uninspiring spec sheet, with just 15 minutes of flight range out of a 110 kWh battery and a cruising speed of 120 km/h, isn't likely to be indicative of what Airbus is really up to behind the scenes, or of what the company might bring to the table when it inevitably gets involved in the air taxi game at a commercial level. We'd imagine that Airbus will pop up with a larger, transitioning eVTOL at some point in the next couple of years.
    Israel's CityHawk should probably not be on this list, because it's not electric, but this bizarre design will eventually be converted to use a hydrogen fuel cell system, and it's certainly an interesting aircraft. It's based on the  Cormorant airframe from Tactical Robotics, formerly known as the AirMule, which has done plenty of testing to validate its weird take on things.
 The  current, turbofan-powered CityHawk design uses two big, fully shielded and allegedly slow-rotating fans at the front and rear of a roughly car-shaped body to handle lift. Forward thrust is provided, on the Cormorant at least, by an additional pair of horizontal ducted fans at the rear. We're at a bit of a loss to understand how it balances itself using this configuration, where thrust sources seem grouped together along a single axis instead of out wide like on most designs, but it certainly seems to fly, even if the demo flights have looked a tad wobbly.
 Notably, this design can get six people aboard, using a significantly smaller footprint than anything else on this list. It's about the width of a car, if a little longer, and Urban Aeronautics says that small footprint will give this thing a much wider choice of landing spots, as well as the ability to park lots of them close together.
 Moving to a hydrogen fuel cell powertrain, which is very much on the roadmap, will allow two contra-rotating fans at each end, and should greatly increase range for what is essentially a drone-like design with no wing assistance. The claimed 168 mph top speed seems a tad optimistic, but we'll wait and see.
    Now fully owned by Boeing,  Aurora Flight Sciences and its PAV Passenger Air Vehicle represent another finger Boeing has stuck in the eVTOL pie, albeit with a strangely similar design to Wisk's Cora. Again, it very much looks like an aeroplane with a VTOL system built in, although this time the VTOL props run front to back on what almost looks like a set of landing skids, and the wings are unfettered. The pusher prop slots in neatly behind.
 Like the Cora, it's conceived to be fully autonomous, and Aurora has two- and four-seat versions in development. That's about the shape of what we know at this stage, other than the fact that Aurora has signed up to partner with the Uber Elevate program alongside some other names on this list.
  Transitioning 10-rotor multicopter with two forward props, 2/4 seats, 155 mph top speed, 186-mile range
  You may best know Hoversurf as the team of Russian psychopaths behind the  Scorpion Hoverbike, but this company has ambitions beyond catapulting themselves skywards on the aerial equivalent of dirt bikes. The latest  Project Formula design differs a little from the super-cool jigger above, chiefly in that it now uses ten larger VTOL ducted fans and a pair of pusher props for forward flight. There are also plans for winged and non-winged versions depending on the desired range figures.
 Hoversurf sees tilting props as a health hazard, accounting for 90 percent of the  V-22 Osprey's accidents, so it's happy to keep vertical and forward thrust completely separate. The way the landing skids house the VTOL props, they should be decently out of the way in forward flight. The speed and range figures clearly assume you're talking about the winged version, and the company doesn't seem to have progressed much past small drone-sized prototypes at this point.
  Transitioning multicopter with four tilting rotors, six seats, 150 mph top speed, 62 mile range
  Bell, headquartered in Texas, is a major aircraft manufacturer with a famous history in rocket planes and fighter jets as well as nearly 80 years in the helicopter business. Since 1960, it's been a subsidiary of Textron, which also holds Beechcraft, Hawker and Cessna and posts annual revenues over US$13 billion. As well as producing some of the best known military and civilian helicopters on the market, it was also a partner on the V22 Osprey, among numerous other tilt-rotor projects. You better believe Bell is taking a swing at an air taxi.
 The Nexus 4EX is the latest design we've seen, a large and snazzy-looking machine using a large wing and four big, shrouded, tilting rotors. Its range is not battery-only; at the moment, it also rocks a gas turbine-powered generator. The company has stated it feels multiple powertrain options including hybrids will make it a more attractive product for commercial customers.
 Designs like this, the Karem Butterfly and the CityAirbus are a little worrying, as they offer no redundancy in case of motor failure. We'll see if that changes as things begin to heat up.
  Transitioning multicopter with 8 vtol rotors and 2 forward props, five seats, unknown top speed, unknown range
  Brazil's Embraer is a large manufacturer, primarily of commercial, military and business jets, pulling in US$3-4 billion a year. So while it's no Boeing, this is a well-established company with a 51-year heritage and plenty on the go. EmbraerX, headquartered in Florida, was founded specifically to break into the disr

note:
在做了10年的活跃(超级)用户后，我刚刚删除了我在四方和群组上的账户，超过40000个活跃签到，1,000+张照片，100个给其他用户的小费，以及推特和其他服务使用的无数新增位置数据。
我不想再登记和贡献我的数据了。我不想主动向他们的系统提供我的数据，如果可能的话，甚至是被动的。
既然Foursquare不在乎他们的超级用户花了多少时间来保持Foursquare数据的更新，而机器人们却在用无稽之谈的数据浪费我们的时间，而且他们也几乎抛弃了他们的普通用户，所以我决定离开这个平台，加入OpenStreetMap，为一个真正的众包和开放平台做出贡献。
多亏了Foursquare，我对随机POI非常了解，我将利用这些知识来编辑OSM。现在我正在阅读大量的常见问题解答和维基，因为OSM并非易事，但我非常肯定我会在以后的很多年里做出贡献。
(最初的日记条目是我的推文的副本，从这一次开始：https://twitter.com/pzumk/status/1263504225222492160But我编辑了条目，使其更具可读性，因为我没有意识到这是公开的，哈哈)。
地点：德国下萨克森州汉诺威地区汉诺威中心
note_en:
I have just now deleted my accounts on Foursquare and Swarm after being an active (super) user for 10 years with more than 40000 active check-ins, 1000+ photos, 100s tipps for other users and a innumerable amount of added location data which has been used by Twitter and other services.
 I don’t want to check-in and contribute my data anymore. I don’t want to actively feed their system with my data, if possible not even passively.
 Since Foursquare doesn’t care about the time their super users have been spending to keep the Foursquare-data fresh whilst bots are wasting our time with nonsense data, and since they are also pretty much abandoning their normal users too, I have decided to leave the platform and join OpenStreetMap to contribute to a real crowdsourced and open platform.
 Thanks to Foursquare I know pretty much about random POI and I’m going to use that knowledge to edit the OSM. Right now I’m reading a lot of the FAQs and Wikis because OSM isn’t easy, but I’m pretty sure I’ll contribute for many years.
  (The original diary entry was a copy of my tweets, starting with this once:  https://twitter.com/pzumk/status/1263504225222492160But I have edited the entry to make it more readable as I didn’t realise this is public, haha)
 Location:  Centre, Hanover, Region Hannover, Lower Saxony, Germany
title: 再见，Foursquare，你好，OpenStreetMap
